title: Tsiolkovsky Rocket Equation
content: The ideal delta-v of a rocket is Δv = v_e · ln(m_0 / m_f), where v_e is the effective exhaust velocity and m_0/m_f is the mass ratio.
extended_content: v_e = I_sp · g_0 where I_sp is specific impulse (s) and g_0 = 9.81 m/s².  The equation quantifies the trade-off between propellant fraction and delta-v capability.  Staging allows higher total Δv by discarding empty tankage.
